content.problemStatement:
Chef has recently moved into an apartment. It takes 303030 minutes for Chef to reach office from the apartment.

Chef left for the office XXX minutes before Chef was supposed to reach. Determine whether or not Chef will be able to reach on time.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if Chef will reach on time, `NO` otherwise.


The output is case-insensitive. Thus, the strings `YES`, `yes`, `yeS`, and `Yes` are all considered the same.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the number of minutes Chef leaves before the scheduled arrival time.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line containing "YES" if Chef can reach on time (i.e., X ≥ 30) or "NO" otherwise. The case of the letters does not matter.

sampleTestCases.output:
YES
YES
NO
NO
YES
YES
sampleTestCases.explanation: Chef needs 30 minutes to commute. For X = 30 and 60 he is exactly on time or early, so YES. For X = 14 and 29 he is late, so NO. X = 31 and 42 are enough, so YES.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read X and compare it with the required travel time of 30 minutes. If X is greater than or equal to 30, Chef leaves early enough and we print YES; otherwise we print NO. This is a direct O(1) check per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
